message:
fix(fr-store): function_load enforces upstream library name charset (br-frankenredis-r85v)

Self-review caught a fourth real defect in the FUNCTION load path:
function_load accepted any UTF-8 string after 'name=' as the
library name, including dashes, dots, exclamation marks, slashes,
and non-ASCII characters. Upstream functions.c::functionsVerifyName
restricts library names to [A-Za-z0-9_] and rejects everything
else with the canonical wording 'Library names can only contain
letters, numbers, or underscores(_) and must be at least one
character long' (functions.c:250 and 977).

Add the byte-set gate after the existing empty-name check.
Mirrors upstream's restriction so a forged shebang like
'#!lua name=evil/path' fails loudly instead of registering an
unsafe library identifier.

New test function_load_rejects_invalid_library_name_chars_with_upstream_wording
walks 5 forbidden cases (dashes, dots, exclamation, accented
char, slash) and confirms a valid alphanumeric+underscore name
still loads.

fr-store 293 passed; fr-command 656 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r85v.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
